title: Tools, Resources, Prompts, and Server Instructions · Retrieval Circuit
summary: Design a minimal MCP surface for an internal knowledge and action service. Learners retrieve rules and procedures before seeing feedback. Planned artifact: Extend the evidence record for an MCP primitive and naming contract after a delayed changed case. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal debugging, and evidence defense.
objectives: Expose actions as narrow tools with descriptive schemas and side-effect annotations.
Expose readable addressable context as resources or templates rather than action tools.
Expose reusable user-controlled prompt templates only when they improve discovery and composition.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

starterFiles.python:
"""Local MCP protocol and tool-contract portfolio."""

steps.title: Separate evidence from confidence
steps.instruction:
Use a changed review case derived from this module: agent ext mcp primitives. Preserve inputs, constraints, an observable trace, and a boundary result.

Inspect the Primitive Choice trace for this review case. Which record connects a precondition, observed state change, and boundary result?
steps.why: The decision supplies observable evidence for choose the smallest primitive that preserves consent, discoverability, caching, and semantics.

content.text: For Primitive Choice, distinguish a claim from evidence by locating the relevant precondition, trace or invariant, and changed input result.

hints: Name the Primitive Choice precondition or invariant that the review case must preserve.
Trace this concrete case before choosing: Use a changed review case derived from this module: agent ext mcp primitives. Preserve inputs, constraints, an observable trace, and a boundary result.
Use the documented misconception as a counterexample target: Tools, resources, and prompts are interchangeable.

stimulus.title: Primitive Choice case record
stimulus.caption: Three excerpts from a learner evidence packet; only one connects a claim to a reproducible check.

lines.label: assumption
lines.text: Use a changed review case derived from this module: agent ext mcp primitives. Preserve inputs, constraints, an observable trace, and a boundary result.

lines.label: risk
lines.text: Tools, resources, and prompts are interchangeable.

lines.label: evidence
lines.text: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Choose the smallest primitive that preserves consent, discoverability, caching, and semantics. Artifact under review: an MCP primitive and naming contract.

options.text: The record names the assumption, applies Primitive Choice, and reports a changed-case check tied to the stakeholder decision.
The record says the result looks right but supplies no quantity, trace, condition, or counterexample.
Apply the visible technique immediately, omit assumptions, and treat one successful output as complete proof.

content.text: Check preconditions, perform the operation in an inspectable order, then verify the result and a boundary case.

options.text: Define the stakeholder decision, quantities, authority, and constraints
Choose and document the smallest defensible model or procedure
Execute the model while preserving inspectable intermediate evidence
Test boundaries and changed cases, communicate limits, and revise

steps.title: Record what required effort
steps.instruction: Write a concise evidence note that explains when Primitive Choice applies, what can fail, and how the changed case was verified.
steps.why: Retrieval and explanation expose gaps that recognition questions can hide.

content.title: Evidence defense
content.text: Use the case, one assumption, one observable result, and one limitation. Do not copy the competency statement: Choose the smallest primitive that preserves consent, discoverability, caching, and semantics.

checks.description: The evidence cycle runs from problem framing through changed-case validation.

checks.description: The evidence note explains the model, failure risk, and verification in the learner’s own words.
checks.failureMessage: Add a causal explanation using choose and smallest plus specific case evidence.
